tasks.title: Add runner support for 'pi' CLI
evidence: Pi CLI binary 'pi' is mentioned in runner_cli_inventory.sh
Runner enum in contracts/config.rs already has Pi variant defined
Basic implementation exists in runner.rs and execution/runners.rs
CLI help text does not list 'pi' as an allowed runner: 'Allowed runners: codex, opencode, gemini, claude, cursor'
runner_cli_inventory.sh already includes 'pi' in its inventory list
User has not used pi before - behavior with --model flag is unknown
Missing: CLI argument parsing, help text updates, documentation updates, model handling verification
plan: Update CLI help text in cli/mod.rs to include 'pi' in allowed runners list
Update CLI help text in cli/run.rs to include 'pi' in allowed runners list
Update CLI help text in cli/task.rs to include 'pi' examples
Update CLI help text in cli/scan.rs to include 'pi' examples
Ensure --runner pi is accepted by argument parser (Runner enum value)
Set default model to 'gpt-5.2' for Pi runner in default_model_for_runner
Update runner.rs default_model_for_runner to return Model::Custom('gpt-5.2') for Pi
Update cli_spec.rs apply_pi_options to pass --model gpt-5.2 by default
Note: Pi does NOT have --yolo flag - use --print (-p) for non-interactive mode
Update doctor.rs to ensure pi binary detection works correctly
Update README.md 'Runners' section to include Pi with usage examples
Update docs/cli.md to include Pi runner documentation
Run 'make ci' to validate changes
notes: Default model for Pi should be 'gpt-5.2' (user specified this as default)
Pi does NOT have --yolo equivalent - it uses --print (-p) for non-interactive mode which auto-approves actions
Pi default model is 'gemini-2.5-flash' per its help - we override to gpt-5.2
Test with: ralph run one --runner pi after implementation
runner_cli_inventory.sh output can help determine pi's CLI interface
tasks.request: Add runner support for 'pi' CLI. Default model should be 'gpt-5.2'. Note: Pi does NOT have a --yolo flag - it uses --print (-p) for non-interactive mode. The runner infrastructure exists but needs to be fully exposed in CLI arguments, help text, and documentation.

tasks.title: Add color support to CLI and TUI for enhanced user experience
evidence: User request: 'we should be using colors in the CLI and TUI whenever possible'
User context: Colors should guide users to important information during long 'ralph run loop' tasks
User context: Current output can be bland white text in black box
User suggestion: Color-code agent reasoning/thinking blocks, tool calls, supervisor calls
User constraint: Don't colorize everything like a preschool art class - find balance
plan: Audit current CLI output for opportunities to add semantic coloring
Audit TUI rendering for color enhancement opportunities
Design color palette: success (green), error (red), warning (yellow), info (blue), emphasis (cyan/magenta)
Add color support for agent reasoning/thinking blocks (subtle gray/blue)
Add color support for tool calls (distinct color like cyan)
Add color support for supervisor/system messages (distinct color like magenta)
Add color support for task status indicators (todo/doing/done)
Add --no-color flag and NO_COLOR env var support for accessibility
Ensure colors work well on both light and dark terminal backgrounds
Run 'make ci' to validate changes
tasks.request: we should be using colors in the CLI and TUI whenever possible to enhance the user experience. Users should be guided by colors, especially but not limited to when long 'ralph run loop' tasks are ongoing, so they can focus on the important information and not get clouded by verbosity. I think the verbosity is good right now but can sometimes be bland when a user runs 'ralph run loop' and sees nothing but white text inside a black box. Maybe we look to color code agent reasoning/thinking blocks, tool calls, supervisor calls, etc? We dont have to colorize everything to make it look like a preschool art class but a nice balance might be beneficial for user expereince.

tasks.title: Unify CLI argument patterns: make 'ralph scan' accept positional prompt like 'ralph task'
evidence: User reports: 'ralph task' works by appending prompt without a flag, but 'ralph scan' requires '--focus' flag
This creates inconsistent UX between two similar commands
Both commands support '--runner' and '--effort' flags, so the prompt syntax should align
plan: Review current 'ralph task' argument parsing to understand how it accepts positional prompt
Review current 'ralph scan' argument parsing and '--focus' flag implementation
Modify 'ralph scan' to accept an optional positional prompt argument (in addition to --focus for backward compatibility)
Update help text and examples for 'ralph scan' to show both usage patterns
Update docs/cli.md to reflect the unified syntax
Run 'make ci' to validate changes
tasks.request: 'ralph task' works by just appending the prompt to the 'ralph task' command without a flag. 'ralph scan' requires the prompt to follow after '--focus'. this seems like an inconsistent user experience. i do use both with different '--runner' and '--effort' flags but it seems odd to have to specify '--focus' for the 'scan' command.

tasks.title: Add scan mode for feature discovery/innovation vs code hygiene
evidence: User reports: 'ralph scan' defaults to looking for repo rules violations, inconsistent/incomplete code (break-fix maintenance)
User wants ability to scan for new features to add, feature completeness for specific use-cases
Current behavior stays focused on code hygiene even when directed toward innovation
Need to balance without overcorrecting away from the useful maintenance mode
plan: Review current scan prompt template to understand how it guides the model
Review existing scan flags to see if there's already a mode toggle
Design a '--mode' or similar flag with values like 'maintenance' (default) and 'innovation'
Create separate prompt paths or conditional prompt sections for each mode
Ensure 'maintenance' mode preserves current break-fix behavior
Ensure 'innovation' mode guides model toward feature gaps, use-case completeness, and enhancement opportunities
Update CLI help text with examples for both modes
Update docs/cli.md with mode descriptions
Run 'make ci' to validate changes
tasks.request: right now when 'ralph scan' is run i think it defaults to looking for repo rules violations, inconsistent or incomplete code, etc. More of a break-fix maintenance order. This works well. But it would be nice to be able to have it look for new features to add. maybe the prompt already has this verbiage for innovation in it, or maybe there is already a flag for it that i have forgotten about, but even when i use the '--focus' and direct the model to look for feature completeness, adding new features for a specific use-case or in general, it seems to stay focused on the code hygiene as aspect much more. Not sure how to handle this without overcorrecting.

tasks.title: Fix TUI terminal resize handling and visual glitches
evidence: User reports: TUI formatting breaks on terminal resize until next update
In some cases formatting remains broken even after updates
Logs need to stream and display properly
User has 'GREAT success with the CLI and barely touch the TUI due to poor user experience'
This is a polish issue that affects user adoption
plan: Review current TUI event handling to understand resize signal processing
Review current rendering logic to identify why redraw doesn't happen immediately on resize
Implement proper terminal resize event handling that triggers immediate full redraw
Ensure layout calculations use current terminal dimensions after resize
Test log streaming display during and after resize operations
Review and fix any remaining visual glitches (flickering, misalignment, truncation)
Test across common terminal emulators (iTerm2, Terminal.app, Alacritty, etc.)
Run 'make ci' to validate changes
tasks.request: we must review the TUI resizing to ensure that when a user resizes their terminal that the screen is redrawn. currently i see a whole lot of formatting break and look like crap until the next update is sent to the screen, and in some cases it still is busted. this is a tough problem to solve, tuis are hard. but we must make sure we have a tui that is performant and without visual glitches. logs need to stream and be displayed properly. user flows and experience need to be polished otherwise it looks like amateur work and users wont want to use it. personally, i have had GREAT success with the CLI and barely touch the TUI due to poor user experience. The CLI version of the 'ralph task', 'ralph scan', and 'ralph run' commands have been excellent.

tasks.title: Add progress indicators and spinners for long-running operations
evidence: User reports: 'verbosity is good but can be bland when running ralph run loop and sees nothing but white text in black box'
Current state: No visual feedback during runner execution phases
Current state: No spinner/progress indication when agent is 'thinking' or processing
UX gap: Users don't know if the system is stuck or working during long operations
Modern CLI expectation: Tools like cargo, npm, docker all show progress indicators
plan: Design progress indicator system: spinners for indeterminate work, progress bars for determinate work
Add phase-aware progress indicators (Planning: ▶, Implementation: ⚙, Review: 👁)
Implement animated spinners for runner thinking/processing states
Add elapsed time display for each phase and total execution
Show current operation description (e.g., 'Running CI gate...', 'Generating plan...')
Ensure progress indicators work in both TUI and CLI modes
Add --no-progress flag for CI/scripting environments
Respect NO_COLOR environment variable for progress indicators
Run 'make ci' to validate changes
notes: Consider using indicatif crate for progress bars if not already used
Progress indicators should not interfere with log output streaming
TUI mode can use richer visuals than CLI mode
tasks.request: Add progress indicators and spinners to make long-running 'ralph run loop' operations feel more responsive and modern. Users should see visual feedback when the agent is thinking, when CI is running, and what phase is currently active. This addresses the 'white text in black box' problem where users can't tell if progress is being made.

tasks.title: Add Kanban board view to TUI for modern task visualization
evidence: Current TUI is list-only which feels dated compared to modern tools
User feedback: TUI has poor user experience compared to CLI commands
Modern expectation: Tools like Linear, GitHub Projects, Jira use Kanban boards
Visual columns provide better task status overview than lists
Blocker: RQ-0398 (TUI resize handling) should be fixed first for good Kanban experience
plan: Design Kanban board layout with columns: Backlog (draft/todo), In Progress (doing), Review (optional), Done
Add view toggle: 'l' for list view (current), 'b' for board view (new)
Implement horizontal column layout with vertical task lists
Add keyboard navigation: Left/Right to switch columns, Up/Down to navigate tasks within column
Show task count badge on each column header
Implement drag-and-drop preparation (visual feedback for move operations)
Ensure board view handles terminal resize gracefully (depends on RQ-0398)
Add color-coded cards based on priority (critical=red, high=orange, medium=yellow, low=green)
Show task ID and truncated title on cards
Update help overlay with board view shortcuts
Run 'make ci' to validate changes
notes: Board view should respect terminal width - fall back to list on narrow terminals
Consider async loading if many tasks affect rendering performance
Future enhancement: Custom column configuration
tasks.request: Add a modern Kanban board view to the TUI alongside the current list view. Users should be able to toggle between list view ('l') and board view ('b'). The board should show columns for different task statuses (Backlog, In Progress, Done) with color-coded cards by priority. This brings Ralph in line with modern project management tools like Linear, GitHub Projects, and Jira, making task visualization much more intuitive.

tasks.title: Add desktop notifications for task completion and failures
evidence: Long-running 'ralph run loop' tasks can take minutes or hours
Users switch contexts during long operations and miss completion
No current mechanism to alert user when work is done
Modern developer tools (Slack, Discord, Docker Desktop) use native notifications
macOS: Notification Center, Windows: Action Center, Linux: D-Bus notifications
plan: Research cross-platform notification libraries (notify-rust for Linux, mac-notification-sys for macOS, windows-notification for Windows)
Add notification configuration to AgentConfig: notifications_enabled, notify_on_complete, notify_on_fail
Implement notification dispatcher with platform detection
Send notification on: task completion (success), task failure, loop completion
Include task ID, title, and status in notification
Add notification grouping to prevent spam during loop mode
Add --notify flag and --no-notify flag for CLI control
Respect system Do Not Disturb settings where possible
Add notification permission handling (graceful degradation if denied)
Run 'make ci' to validate changes
notes: Notification should only trigger when user is not actively viewing the TUI
Consider adding sound notifications as optional enhancement
Batch notifications for multiple task completions in loop mode
tasks.request: Add cross-platform desktop notifications that alert users when tasks complete or fail. This is essential for long-running 'ralph run loop' operations where users may switch to other work. Notifications should work on macOS (Notification Center), Windows (Action Center), and Linux (D-Bus). Include task ID, title, and completion status. Add configuration options to enable/disable notifications and control when they fire.

tasks.title: Implement smart task templates for common development patterns
evidence: Common patterns recur: 'Add tests for X', 'Refactor Y for performance', 'Fix error handling in Z'
Current workflow requires typing full request every time
No way to quickly create tasks with pre-filled scope, tags, and plan structure
Templates would speed up task creation significantly
Similar to GitHub issue templates or IDE snippets
plan: Define built-in template library: add-tests, refactor-performance, fix-error-handling, add-docs, security-audit
Add TaskTemplate struct with title pattern, default tags, default scope patterns, plan template
Create templates.json or embed templates in binary
Add ralph task --template <name> <target> command
Support template variables: {{target}}, {{module}}, {{file}}
Add ralph task list-templates command to show available templates
Allow custom templates in .ralph/templates/ directory
Auto-detect context (current file, git branch) to pre-fill template variables
Update task builder prompts to understand template context
Run 'make ci' to validate changes
notes: Templates should integrate with existing ralph task command seamlessly
Consider interactive template selection when --template not specified but templates available
Template system should be extensible for user-defined templates
tasks.request: Implement a smart task template system for common development patterns. Built-in templates like 'add-tests', 'refactor-performance', 'fix-error-handling' should pre-fill task fields (tags, scope, plan structure). Users can run 'ralph task --template add-tests src/module.rs' to quickly create well-structured tasks. This dramatically speeds up task creation and ensures consistency in task structure.

tasks.title: Add task completion celebration and productivity stats
evidence: Task completion is anticlimactic - no feedback or celebration
Dopamine feedback loops increase tool engagement (see: GitHub contributions graph, Todoist karma)
Users don't have visibility into their productivity trends
No session summary after completing multiple tasks
Small celebrations make CLI tools feel more polished and human
plan: Add celebration animation for task completion (optional, can be disabled)
Implement session stats: tasks completed this session, time saved, streak counter
Add daily/weekly productivity dashboard in TUI (press 's' for stats view)
Show milestone celebrations (10 tasks, 50 tasks, 100 tasks completed)
Add 'completion summary' after run loop finishes with task list and stats
Implement velocity tracking: tasks per day/week trend
Store productivity metrics in .ralph/cache/stats.json
Add ralph stats command to view productivity reports
Ensure celebrations don't interfere with scripting/CI use cases
Run 'make ci' to validate changes
notes: Celebrations should be subtle and professional, not overly flashy
Stats should respect privacy - local only, no external reporting
Consider ASCII art celebrations for terminal compatibility
tasks.request: Add task completion celebrations and productivity stats to make Ralph more engaging. Small dopamine hits on task completion (subtle animation, confetti in TUI), session summaries showing tasks completed and time spent, streak counters, and velocity tracking. This gamification makes the tool more enjoyable to use while giving users visibility into their productivity patterns. Include ralph stats command for detailed productivity reports.

tasks.title: Implement watch mode for automatic task detection and creation
evidence: Developers often leave TODO/FIXME comments that get forgotten
Test failures should automatically suggest fix tasks
File changes can indicate areas needing attention
No current mechanism for proactive task suggestions
Watch mode is common in modern dev tools (cargo watch, jest --watch)
plan: Add notify or similar file watching dependency
Implement ralph watch command with configurable watch patterns
Detect TODO/FIXME/HACK comments in changed files and offer to create tasks
Monitor test output and suggest tasks for failing tests
Watch for compiler errors and offer fix tasks
Add --auto-queue flag to automatically create tasks without prompting
Add --watch-patterns flag for custom file patterns
Implement debouncing to avoid task spam during active editing
Add notification when new tasks are auto-created
Run 'make ci' to validate changes
notes: Watch mode should be efficient - use git ignore patterns
Consider integration with existing scan functionality
Should not interfere with normal development workflow
tasks.request: Implement ralph watch command that monitors the codebase and automatically suggests or creates tasks. Detect TODO/FIXME comments in modified files, suggest tasks for failing tests, and monitor for patterns that indicate work needed. Similar to cargo watch or jest --watch but focused on task creation. Helps capture work items that would otherwise be forgotten during development.

tasks.title: Add fuzzy search for tasks with improved discovery
evidence: Current ralph queue search requires exact substring match
Users often forget exact task IDs or wording
Fuzzy search improves discoverability significantly
Modern tools (fzf, VS Code quick open) use fuzzy matching
Helps when task list grows large (100+ tasks)
plan: Research fuzzy matching libraries (nucleo, skim, or custom implementation)
Add --fuzzy flag to ralph queue search command
Implement fuzzy matching across: title, tags, scope, evidence, request fields
Add relevance scoring and sort results by match quality
Add ralph task find command as shorthand for fuzzy search
Integrate fuzzy search into TUI with '/' keybinding (enhance existing search)
Highlight matching characters in results
Support combined filters: fuzzy search + status + tag filters
Maintain backward compatibility with exact search as default
Run 'make ci' to validate changes
notes: Fuzzy search should be fast even with large task lists
Consider using existing nucleo matcher if already in dependency tree
TUI integration should feel natural and responsive
tasks.request: Add fuzzy search capability to ralph queue search for better task discovery. Current search requires exact substring matches, making it hard to find tasks when you don't remember exact wording. Fuzzy search should match across title, tags, scope, and description fields with relevance scoring. Add --fuzzy flag to search command and integrate into TUI search (enhance the '/' keybinding). This is essential for large task lists where exact matching becomes cumbersome.

tasks.title: Add session recovery and crash resilience for long-running loops
evidence: If Ralph crashes during a run loop, progress is lost
No way to resume interrupted multi-task sessions
User must manually track which tasks completed before crash
Critical for long-running loops (hours of work)
Modern expectation: Tools auto-save and recover state
plan: Add session state tracking: current_task_id, phase, completed_tasks, remaining_tasks
Write session state to .ralph/cache/session.json before each task
Update session state after each task completion
Add ralph run resume command to continue interrupted session
On startup, detect incomplete session and prompt to resume
Implement session timeout (sessions older than N hours require confirmation)
Add --resume flag to ralph run loop for explicit resume
Handle edge cases: task no longer exists, queue changed since crash
Clear session file on clean completion
Run 'make ci' to validate changes
notes: Session recovery should be resilient to queue modifications
Consider git state snapshot for advanced recovery
Should work with --max-tasks limit (count remaining from crash point)
tasks.request: Implement session recovery and crash resilience for ralph run loop. Currently, if Ralph crashes during a long-running loop, all progress is lost and the user must manually track which tasks completed. Add session state tracking that writes to .ralph/cache/session.json before each task, enabling ralph run resume command to continue from where the crash occurred. Prompt on startup if an incomplete session is detected. Critical for production use where loops may run for hours.
